description:
Goal: make the compiler2 native handoff truthful and self-sufficient before moving more backend code. Signal: the compiler2-native backend contract is expressed directly in compiler2-owned data structures; no compiler2-native consumer needs synthetic `SpecKey`/`SpecPlan`/`SpecRegistry`, widened `return_tys`, or old-world semantic payloads hung off `fz_ir` just to answer backend questions.

Change:
- audit each compiler2-native backend question and make the answer live directly on `NativeProgram` / `NativeBody` or another compiler2-owned artifact
- remove or replace old-world semantic payloads that are only present to feed the planner-shaped native route, especially extern contracts and dispatch/type-test payloads
- tighten contract tests around the handoff so the backend surface is correct by construction before the consumer move

Why:
- if the handoff still encodes old-world assumptions, copying codegen in-house just relocates the rot

Checks:
- compiler2 artifact tests prove the backend reads direct compiler2 facts for body identity, value types, callable entries, continuation ownership, extern metadata, and dispatch/type-test inputs
- the remaining compiler2-native backend questions can be answered without inventing planner state
title: Compiler2 native backends: make the native handoff truthful and self-sufficient
